_note: Calibrated for a freshman/sophomore audience. Includes questions moved up from the source's 'easy'/'medium' tiers because they need advanced design (multi-stack/trie/O(1) structures), 2D dynamic programming, topological sort, or non-recursive tree traversal. `requires` lists assumed background, shown in the UI.

questions.title: Inorder Traversal
questions.prompt: Given a binary search tree, print out the elements of the tree in order without using recursion.

questions.title: Tree Level Order
questions.prompt: Given a tree, write a function that prints out the nodes of the tree in level order.

questions.title: Square Submatrix
questions.prompt: Given a 2D array of 1s and 0s, find the largest square subarray of all 1s.

questions.title: N Stacks
questions.prompt: Implement N > 0 stacks using a single array to store all stack data. No stack should be full unless the entire array is full.

examples.input: 2 stacks sharing one array, fill both until the array is full
examples.output: only then is a push rejected

questions.title: Build Order
questions.prompt: Given a list of packages that need to be built and the dependencies for each package, determine a valid order in which to build the packages.

questions.title: Random Binary Tree
questions.prompt: Implement a binary tree with a method getRandomNode() that returns a random node.

examples.input: tree with nodes {1,2,3,4,5}, call getRandomNode() many times
examples.output: each node returned ~20% of the time (uniformly random)

questions.title: Tree to Doubly Linked List
questions.prompt: Given a tree, write a function to convert it into a circular doubly linked list from left to right by only modifying the existing pointers.

questions.title: Longest Consecutive Branch
questions.prompt: Given a tree, write a function to find the length of the longest branch of nodes in increasing consecutive order.

questions.title: Max Stacks
questions.prompt: Implement a LIFO stack that has push(), pop(), and max() functions, where max() returns the maximum value in the stack. All of these functions should run in O(1) time.

questions.title: Autocomplete
questions.prompt: Write an autocomplete class that returns all dictionary words with a given prefix.

examples.input: dict {abc, acd, bcd, def, a, aba}, prefix "a"

questions.title: Median of Arrays
questions.prompt: Find the median of two sorted arrays.

questions.title: 0-1 Knapsack
questions.prompt: Given a list of items with values and weights, as well as a max weight, find the maximum value you can generate from items where the sum of the weights is less than the max.

questions.title: Matrix Product
questions.prompt: Given a matrix, find the path from top left to bottom right with the greatest product by moving only down and right.

questions.title: Shortest Path
questions.prompt: Given a directed graph, find the shortest path between two nodes if one exists.

questions.title: Big Int Modules
questions.prompt: Given a list of bytes a, each representing one byte of a larger integer (e.g. {0x12,0x34,0x56,0x78} represents 0x12345678), and an integer b, find a % b.

questions.title: Random Linked List
questions.prompt: Given a linked list where each node has two pointers, one to the next node and one to a random node in the list, clone the linked list.

examples.input: A -> B -> C, where A.random = C and B.random = A
examples.output: a deep copy A' -> B' -> C' with A'.random = C' and B'.random = A'

questions.title: String Deletion
questions.prompt: Given a string and a dictionary HashSet, write a function to determine the minimum number of characters to delete to make a word.

questions.title: Longest Common Substring
questions.prompt: Given two strings, write a function that returns the longest common substring.
